$statusLegend.ported: byte-equivalent to upstream (CI fails on drift)
$statusLegend.adapted: TypeScript port and/or namespace swap; logically equivalent
$statusLegend.extended: fork adds behavior on top of upstream
$statusLegend.added: file unique to the fork (no upstream)

files.notes: name + textdomain swapped to the artisanpack namespace; attribute schema, supports, usesContext/providesContext carried verbatim for parity.

Previews via the lightweight createEntityPlaceholderEdit helper (renders the stamped _resolved* value when present, otherwise a labelled placeholder). Upstream edit.js is intentionally NOT delegated for the post-*/site-* display blocks: it queries the page/site/user entity through @wordpress/core-data, which the post editor does not populate, so upstream renders empty (site-*) or crashes (post-author unlocks block-editor private APIs). Front-end markup is produced server-side from the stamped _resolved* attributes.
Dynamic block — returns null; markup produced server-side from stamped _resolved* attributes.
Bidirectional core/post-title ↔ artisanpack/post-title block transforms for the rollout window.
Inline SVG inserter icon (editor lacks dashicons.css).
Adapted to the visual-editor auto-discovery contract.
Attribute-migration entries ported to TypeScript (save returns null — block is server-rendered).

knownDivergences: edit.tsx previews via createEntityPlaceholderEdit rather than upstream edit.js — the core-data shim does not expose the page/site/user entity to the post editor, so the upstream entity-querying edit renders empty or crashes. Live in-editor entity values would require expanding the shim (out of scope for the fork).
post-title is server-rendered: the front-end markup is produced by the Blade/React/Vue renderers from stamped _resolved* attributes, not by save().
transforms.ts adds bidirectional block transforms that do not exist upstream — required to coexist with core/post-title during the V1 rollout.
